 ## Historical Pricing
-- Week 4: A=$9, B=$15
-- Week 5-8: A=$9, B=$19 (C varied, ended at $49)
-- Week 10: A=$12 (FAILED - didn't save, reverted to $9)
-- Week 12: A=$19, B=$39, C=$79 (with S1 promos to net $9/$19/$44)
+- Weeks 4-8: A=$9, B=$19 (worked, good conversion before competitor events)
+- Weeks 9-10: Conversion dropped as competitor events raised q_min
+- Week 11-12: 0% conversion at T1/T2/T3
+- Week 12: Emergency pivot to T3/T4/T5 with $19/$39/$79 + heavy promos
+- Week 13: Back to T2/T3/T3 with $9/$19/$149, minimal promos
 
-## Lessons Learned (IMPORTANT)
-- Plan C at T5 is marginally profitable at best for S1 (compute/seat is huge)
-- S2/S3/E1/E2/E3 are all unreachable segments
-- 0% conversion = all ad spend is wasted. FIX CONVERSION FIRST, then spend on ads
-- Competitor events permanently raise q_min - must track and respond
-- set_prices doesn't change existing subs' listed_price until re-billing
-- Promotion structure is additive: global + by_group + by_customer + by_group_plan
-- Tenure loyalty helps existing subs tolerate quality drift (+0.13 at 12 months)
-- In-app ads at 0.5 strength generate ~$7/day with quality penalty
-- Quality formula: (base + shared_bonus + group_bonus) × tier_multiplier
-- Compute cost scales LINEARLY with both usage AND model tier cost
-- Dev spending has LOGARITHMIC returns - can't meaningfully increase quality via dev alone
-- Need model tiers OR R&D for significant quality boosts
+## Lessons Learned
+- Stacking promos can create $0 effective price subs (29 subs!) - AVOID
+- Compute cost scales linearly with tier - T5 at 0.03/unit is 100x T1 at 0.0003
+- S1 alone CANNOT sustain the business - margins too thin, WTP too low
+- S3 is the best expansion target: high WTP, reachable q_min, profitable per sub
+- Dev spending has logarithmic returns - not viable for meaningful quality boost
+- R&D minimum cost: $166K for tier 1 - far beyond current cash
+- Quotas can segment customers - force high-usage S3 to premium plans
+- Competitor events permanently raise quality expectations
+- log_rationale is part of the next-week CLI command, not a separate API call
 
-## Key Metrics to Watch Next Week
-1. **Compute cost** - CRITICAL. If >$100/day, must revert tiers
-2. **Conversion rate** - should recover from 0% if quality > q_min
-3. **Churn rate** - existing subs face new prices at billing
-4. **New leads** - with reduced ads, expect fewer leads
-5. **Downgrades** - should decrease with much higher quality
+## Current Burn Rate
+- Projected: ~$630-700/week
+- Runway: ~15 weeks (to week ~28)
+- Game length: 71 weeks (497 days)
+- MUST find profitability or dramatically slow burn
 
-## Strategy for Week 13+
-1. Monitor compute cost closely - adjust tiers if unsustainable
-2. If conversion resumes: slowly increase ad spend
-3. If conversion still 0%: q_min still above our quality - NEED R&D (impossible w/ current cash)
-4. Consider: increase quotas to attract higher-value users
-5. Consider: reduce ads_strength from 0.5 (saves quality penalty)
-6. LONG-TERM: need to reach other segments somehow
